EUR 1 of NAV growth at Ferrari translates to roughly EUR 0.50 of EXO price; if the discount even normalizes to
its 10-year average of ~35%, EXO re-rates ~25% before any NAV growth. Buybacks at half NAV are ~2x
accretive per share retired.
thesis:
Buy Ferrari, Stellantis, Philips and Iveco at roughly half-price via the Agnelli family vehicle; ~52% discount to
GAV with active buybacks.
WHY MISPRICED
Persistent ~50% conglomerate/holdco discount despite single-asset Ferrari already worth ~70% of mcap. Drivers:
cyclical autos (Stellantis), private/illiquid sleeves, foreign listing complexity (Amsterdam-listed, OTC ADR),
Agnelli family control through Giovanni Agnelli BV reduces takeover optionality.
EDGE
Discount is now wider than 10-yr average of ~35% even after a EUR 1B buyback funded by Ferrari sell-down in
Feb 2025. Each EUR of NAV growth at Ferrari translates only ~50 cents into Exor price -- pure free option on
discount narrowing.
whatScreenCantSee:
The Iveco-to-Tata transaction (announced July 30, 2025) hands Exor ~EUR 3.8B of cash mid-2026 — recycled
into either a new EUR-billion buyback or healthcare deployment. John Elkann's letters increasingly emphasize
Lingotto, which now owns stakes in Christian Louboutin, Shang Xia, Welltec, and a venture book — a future
Lingotto IPO would crystallize ~EUR 4B of currently-marked-at-fund-NAV value. The Stellantis CEO transition
(Antonio Filosa took over in mid-2025 after Carlos Tavares departed) creates execution risk but also turnaround
optionality. Note also: Exor took a stake in Royal Philips at distressed levels in 2022-23-25 and is now the largest
shareholder ahead of any margin recovery. The OTC EXOSF symbol is stale — execute via EXXRF (more liquid
OTC ADR) or the Amsterdam EXO listing for any size.
catalyst:
• Iveco-Tata sale closes (mid-2026), ~EUR 3.8B cash inflow
• FY2025 Annual Report and any new buyback announcement (March 2026 cycle)
• Stellantis Capital Markets Day under new CEO (H1 2026)

name: Vivendi SE (post-spin remaining stub) — ADR
analystSummary:
Post-split Vivendi is a near-pure listed-stake holdco. The crown jewel is ~10% net economic exposure to
Universal Music Group (UMG) — the world's largest recorded-music company, currently worth EUR ~38B at
UMG.AS. Telecom Italia is a smaller residual stake being progressively trimmed. Gameloft (mobile gaming) is
the only meaningful unlisted asset, marked conservatively. The Bolloré family controls ~29.3% via Compagnie
de l'Odet. Vivendi has self-published monthly NAV since December 2024 to combat info asymmetry, a
corporate-governance positive.
The bear case is the Bolloré overhang and process risk. Bolloré orchestrated the 2024 split partly to escape "de
facto control" rules under French market law. The Court of Cassation in November 2025 overturned the Paris
Court of Appeal finding that Bolloré controls Vivendi — a setback for minority shareholders who had hoped for a
mandatory tender at fair value. The case has been remanded; oral arguments come May 22, 2026. UMG itself has
fallen ~10% YTD 2026 on streaming-growth deceleration, dragging Vivendi NAV. Central run-rate ~EUR
85m/yr is a permanent stub-cost. Gameloft is hard to mark and could disappoint. Liquidity is moderate; VIVHY
ADR is OTC pink with wide spreads.
The bull case is binary catalysts paired with mark-to-market hard NAV. If the May 2026 hearing — or any
subsequent appeal — re-confirms control, Bolloré is forced to launch a mandatory cash offer at a "fair price" that
arbitrators argue should approximate NAV. That alone implies a near-double from current levels. Even without a
tender, continued asset sales (Vivendi has trimmed Telefonica and Telecom Italia in 2024-25) plus the
self-published NAV format compress the discount. UMG's strategic value (Spotify renegotiation, AI music
licensing, possible US listing in 2026) provides NAV-growth optionality. The current ~50% discount to a
transparent, mark-to-market portfolio is the type of asymmetric setup Asset Value Investors (AVI) has been
writing checks for.
thesis:
Trading at ~50% of published NAV after the Canal+/Havas/LHG spin; AMF has ordered Bollore to launch a
mandatory bid -- court ruling pending.
WHY MISPRICED
Bollore family 29.3% controlling stake creates control overhang; central corporate cost run-rate ~EUR 85m/yr;
unlisted Gameloft hard to mark; market doubts that Bollore will pay full NAV in any tender.
EDGE
The catalyst is binary and dated: Court of Cassation hearing on whether Bollore must make a mandatory cash
offer was Nov 25 2025. If Bollore loses appeal it must launch a tender within 6 months at a 'fair price' that
arbitrators argue should be ~NAV. Either tender or continued forced asset sales narrow the discount.
whatScreenCantSee:
The May 22, 2026 Court of Cassation remand hearing is a hard date — minority shareholders Phitrust and Ciam
have been activist on this since 2024, with the AMF originally siding with them (decision later voided). In early
2026, Compagnie de l'Odet (Bolloré's own holdco) sold ~6m UMG shares at EUR 27.50 — a quiet way to extract
value before any forced bid. Vivendi's monthly NAV publication is unique among European holdcos and has
narrowed the discount somewhat since launch but still leaves 50% — historically high. The Gameloft division is
the wild card: rumors of a sale or carve-out have circulated. Note: VIV.PA is the primary line; VIVHY ADR is
OTC pink and quotes can drift relative to Paris. Use limit orders.
catalyst:
• May 22, 2026 — Court of Cassation remand oral arguments
• UMG Q1 2026 earnings + any US dual-listing announcement
• Continued monthly NAV publications + further asset sales

name: Cannae Holdings, Inc.
analystSummary:
Cannae was spun from Fidelity National Financial in 2017 as Bill Foley's standalone investment vehicle. Foley's
track record (FNF, Black Knight, MSG-style M&A; acumen) gives Cannae an unusual proprietary-deal pipeline.
The portfolio has rotated dramatically — from a public-stake bag (D&B;, Ceridian/Dayforce, Alight) into private
operating businesses, with Black Knight Football Club (BKFC) now the cornerstone. BKFC owns 100% of AFC
Bournemouth (Premier League), 100% of FC Lorient (Ligue 1), majority of Moreirense (Portugal), with strategic
affiliations to Auckland FC, Orlando City SC, Kyoto Sanga. TTM revenue at BKFC ~$267m. Cannae
self-publishes monthly SOTP and has been buying back stock aggressively below NAV.
The bear case centers on related-party governance and asset opacity. Trasimene Capital — a Foley-affiliated
management company — collects fees on Cannae assets, a structure activist Carronade explicitly criticized.
Multiple private buckets (BKFC, Restaurant Group, JANA, consumer brands) are hard for outside analysts to
mark, creating a "trust me" valuation discount. European football economics are notoriously cash-burn-heavy;
Premier League survival is not guaranteed. The 2025 AGM saw the management pay proposal rejected — a
positive for shareholders but a sign of structural conflict. Liquidity is thin (~$680m mcap) and float-shrinkage
further reduces tradability.
The bull case is multi-layered. ~$500m returned to shareholders in 21 months and the float retired by ~34% — a
true buyback machine below NAV. The Restaurant Group is under strategic review (read: likely sale). BKFC is
the asymmetric upside — recent European football transactions (Chelsea minority sale at $3B+ EV; Manchester
United partial sale; Premier League franchise valuations rising sharply) suggest a single comp event could
multi-bag the implied football value. Carronade winning board seats means more shareholder-aligned capital
allocation. With ~$200m+ in cash and Treasuries plus public stakes, the implied private-asset value is
meaningfully discounted.
thesis:
Bill Foley vehicle at ~46% discount to disclosed SOTP NAV; aggressive buybacks (~28% of float retired in
2025) plus pivot to private sports/entertainment assets.
WHY MISPRICED
Foley-related-party concerns (Trasimene mgmt agreement criticized by Carronade activist), 'too many private
buckets' concern, sports asset opacity, recent governance loss at 2025 AGM (mgmt pay proposal not approved -- a
positive for shareholders).
EDGE
$500m returned to holders in 21 months; Carronade activist won board seats; Restaurant Group strategic review
(likely sale); BKFC is the main lever -- a single comp transaction in European football (Chelsea, Man U partial
sales at multi-billion EVs) could re-rate the implied football value.
whatScreenCantSee:
The February 23, 2026 SOTP refresh confirmed the BKFC mark at $249m invested for ~42% economics —
implying group EV around $590m. AFC Bournemouth's 2024-25 Premier League finish + new TV deal ramp
could re-rate BKFC alone by 30-50% within 18 months. The Trasimene management agreement is worth reading
in proxy footnotes — Carronade's activist letters argue it transfers ~$10-15m/yr of value to Foley affiliates. The
2025 AGM "no" vote on pay is a rare governance win at a Foley vehicle. Note that JANA Partners' Cannae stake
is itself an interesting kicker — Cannae owns equity in an activist firm. Watkins beverage and Minden Mill
bourbon are early-stage consumer bets; Sightline Payments was written down materially — a reminder that not
every Foley deal works.

name: IAC Inc.
analystSummary:
IAC is Barry Diller's serial-spinout machine — having previously birthed and separated Match Group, Expedia,
LendingTree, Vimeo, Angi, and others. CEO Joey Levin runs the parent. Post-March 2025 Angi spin, IAC is now
a focused holdco with: (1) a ~25% MGM Resorts stake (acquired in 2020-22 at much lower prices, now ~$2.5B);
(2) People Inc. (the rebranded Dotdash Meredith) — owner of People, Better Homes & Gardens, Investopedia,
Allrecipes, Verywell — generating Q4 '25 digital revenue of $355m (+14% YoY) with full-year adj. EBITDA
guide of $310-340m; (3) Care.com, which swung Q4 EBITDA to $19m (+135%); (4) smaller bets in Vivian
Health and the Daily Beast. ~$700m of net cash sits at the parent for opportunistic capital allocation.
The bear case is the chronic conglomerate discount and a digital-ad cycle. People Inc. is the swing factor — print
revenue fell 23% in Q4 '25 and the digital-ad business is sensitive to a recession. Care.com is rebuilding but still
small. The MGM stake's "unlock" thesis depends on Diller actually monetizing — and he tends to wait years.
Print-magazine assets inside People Inc. depress reported multiples vs pure-digital comps. Spin candidates
(People Inc.) are not yet committed. Diller is in his 80s — succession is real risk despite Levin running ops.
The bull case is mathematical. MGM stake ($2.5B) + net cash ($0.7B) = $3.2B vs the entire $3.4B market cap —
which means the rest of IAC (a top-3 US digital publisher generating $300m+ EBITDA) trades at essentially zero.
People Inc. comparables (NYT at ~25x, Ziff Davis at ~7x, Future PLC at ~8x) imply a $2-3B fair value alone.
IAC has spun out 10+ companies; People Inc. is the obvious next candidate once digital growth reliably outpaces
print decline. Levin retired ~10% of float in 2025 ($37m in Q4 alone) and is buying back below SOTP.
MGM-Osaka opens 2030 — long-tail re-rating optionality.
thesis:
Buy MGM Resorts stake plus net cash for free; private digital businesses (Dotdash Meredith, Care.com) thrown
in for nothing.
WHY MISPRICED
Habitual conglomerate discount; Dotdash digital ad cyclicality; market doubts MGM stake is 'unlocked' value;
Angi spin already digested but no clear catalyst for further breakup.
EDGE
MGM stake + cash alone ($3.1B) approximates the entire mcap -- so private digital portfolio is a free option. IAC
has spun out 10 companies; Dotdash Meredith is the next likely spin/sale candidate. Joey Levin still aggressive on
buybacks below SOTP.
whatScreenCantSee:
IAC bought 1m additional MGM shares in Q4 2025 for $40m — Levin still adding to the position despite the
parent trading below MGM-stake-plus-cash. The Dotdash-Meredith integration is officially "complete" as of late
2025 and management has subtly opened the door to a People Inc. spin in the 2026 letter. The $800m senior notes
refinancing at People Inc. in Q1 2025 cleaned up the capital structure ahead of a potential separation. Care.com's
swing to ~$70m+ run-rate EBITDA makes it a legitimate sale candidate to a strategic (e.g., a benefits platform).
Diller's last shareholder letter explicitly framed IAC as "a portfolio of underappreciated assets" — Diller-speak
for "we're going to surface value." Note: the 10-K (filed Feb 20, 2026) discloses Diller still controls Class B
supervotes, so any breakup requires his consent — but his entire career is breakups.
catalyst:
• People Inc. (Dotdash Meredith) spin-off or sale announcement (2026 most likely)
• Care.com sale to a strategic
• Continued buyback tempo + any MGM stake monetization commentary

name: Loews Corporation (Tisch family)
analystSummary:
Loews is a Tisch-family conglomerate (descendants of Larry & Bob Tisch) with the smallest holdco discount of
the five names but the highest quality of disclosed assets. CNA is a mid-sized P&C; and specialty insurer trading
roughly at book — CNA pays Loews ~$700m/yr in dividends including periodic specials. Boardwalk Pipeline is a
pure-play interstate natural-gas system (~14,000 miles, FERC-regulated) that Loews took private in 2018 at a
controversial low; it now generates ~$1B of EBITDA and at FERC-pipe multiples of 11-13x is worth $11-13B.
Loews Hotels operates ~26 hotels including the high-margin Universal Orlando JV — Epic Universe opened in
2025, materially lifting RevPAR and ADR for the JV. Altium Packaging is a plastic-bottle and rigid-packaging
manufacturer.
The bear case is the perpetual Tisch-discount and slow-monetization risk. CNA is sub-scale in a hardening but
consolidation-prone P&C; market — it could be a structural laggard vs Travelers/Chubb. Boardwalk operates in a
regulated, low-growth segment with rate-case overhang and energy-transition headlines. Hotels are cyclical. The
Tisches almost never sell — Boardwalk re-IPO talk has circulated for years without action. Book value/share
growth has been steady but not explosive (~6-9%/yr). The 25-30% discount has persisted for decades; mean
reversion is slow. Insurance-driven NAV is sensitive to catastrophe losses (CNA had elevated cat-loss years
recently).
The bull case is compounding-machine math. Year-end 2025 BVPS (ex-AOCI) was $95.89, up from $88.18 a
year earlier — ~9% growth. Loews bought back 8.9M shares for $782m in 2025 — accretive to BVPS by
mid-single-digits annually. CNA's $700m+ dividend stream funds those buybacks. Boardwalk would IPO at a
premium when rates fall — even a partial 20-30% IPO at infrastructure multiples would crystallize $2-3B of
currently-hidden value. Loews Hotels is the surprise upside — Universal Epic Universe (opened May 2025) is
driving record Orlando attendance and RevPAR; Loews has six on-site Universal hotels with management
agreements. The discount is narrower than peers because the assets are higher-quality and disclosure is cleaner.
thesis:
Tisch-controlled at ~20-30% discount to SOTP; effectively get Boardwalk pipelines and Loews Hotels for free
over CNA stake plus net cash.
WHY MISPRICED
Persistent classic Tisch holdco discount -- has run 15-30% for decades; CNA is a sub-scale P&C; insurer trading
well below book; Boardwalk private hard to mark; market questions 'when' not 'if' on monetization (Tisch family
rarely sells).
EDGE
Strong record of buybacks below NAV (>5% of float per year); CNA dividend stream ~$700m/yr to HoldCo; new
Universal Epic theme park boosts Loews Hotels' Orlando JV value materially through 2026.
whatScreenCantSee:
The Boardwalk Pipeline carrying value on Loews' balance sheet is dramatically below its mark-to-market
private-pipeline equivalent. Loews disclosed in its 2025 10-K (filed Feb 10, 2026) that Boardwalk delivered
"record" results in 2025. Universal Epic Universe (opened May 22, 2025) is Loews Hotels' biggest organic
catalyst:
• Continued aggressive buybacks (~$700-900m/yr pace)
• CNA special dividends + dividend increases

name: Qnity Electronics, Inc.
thesis:
Semiconductor & electronic-materials pure-play spun from DuPont (semi tech + interconnect solutions);
'picks-and-shovels' to AI accelerator and advanced-node semiconductor build-out.
WHY MISPRICED
DuPont generalist chemicals holders sold the higher-multiple electronics stub to fund continued ownership of the
slower industrial chem parent; complex carve-out financials initially obscured run-rate margins. Despite a strong
debut, pricing is still anchored to the spin-distribution price rather than peer multiples.
EDGE
Hidden gem inside DuPont — semiconductor materials franchise has 25%+ EBITDA margins, mid-teens revenue
growth, and supplies consumables for thermal management/EMI shielding/advanced-node — directly levered to
Nvidia/AMD/hyperscaler accelerator builds. Oppenheimer Outperform $100 PT; peer multiples (ENTG, CCMP,
CBT) suggest 30%+ upside re-rating.
catalyst:
• First standalone earnings (Q4 2025)
• S&P; 500 inclusion (already announced)
• AI/advanced-node design-win announcements
• Potential M&A; or buybacks with clean balance sheet

name: The Magnum Ice Cream Company N.V.
thesis:
Global #1 ice-cream pure-play (Magnum, Ben & Jerry's, Wall's, Breyers) demerged from Unilever; brand
portfolio at consumer-staples valuation discount given trapped within slower CPG.
WHY MISPRICED
Unilever holders sold without ever opening a model — too small for the global CPG benchmarks they hold. SEC
delays during US gov't shutdown caused timing confusion. Ben & Jerry's social-issue noise added headline risk
that scares conservative consumer holders. NV / European structure adds friction for US institutional buyers.
EDGE
Standalone, MICC has 20%+ EBITDA margin potential vs. parent's diluted ~14%; pricing power on premium
brands has been demonstrated through 2022-2024 inflation. Strategic suitors (Mondelez, Mars, Nestle) plus
financial sponsors are obvious in $9B-$15B EV range. Recent pricing actions and supply-chain optimization
haven't been re-rated.
catalyst:
• First fully standalone earnings cycle
• Potential strategic M&A; interest
• Margin-expansion roadmap from new mgmt
• US dual listing or ADR re-listing for index inclusion

name: Versant Media Group, Inc.
thesis:
Comcast's 	cable 	network 	portfolio 	(USA, 	MSNBC/MS 	NOW, 	CNBC, 	Syfy, 	E!, 	Golf, 	Oxygen) 	+
Fandango/Rotten Tomatoes spun off; deeply discounted 'melting ice cube' that generated $1.4B net income in
2024.
WHY MISPRICED
Comcast holders are owning broadband + theme parks + Sky, didn't want the linear-cable shrinkage; spin opened
down 14% on day one with indiscriminate selling. Linear TV pessimism is at peak — Versant projected to decline
in 2025 — even though it generates >$1B FCF that funds buybacks/transformation. Tracking PARA/WBD
secular compression makes investors gun-shy.
EDGE
Even with linear declines, the assets generate massive FCF and 80%+ revenue is still profitable; CEO Mark
Lazarus has explicitly signaled M&A; roll-up strategy. Likely active acquirer of CW, AMC Networks or Scripps
assets. Trading at ~5x EBITDA / sub-1x revenue creates significant downside protection plus optionality on
transformation or strategic combination.
catalyst:
• First quarterly FCF print as standalone
• M&A; of complementary cable network
• Buyback authorization

name: Atrium Therapeutics, Inc.
thesis:
RNA-therapeutic spinout from Avidity-Novartis deal with $270M cash and 2 preclinical genetic-cardiomyopathy
programs — trading near cash with all platform value free.
WHY MISPRICED
Created via Novartis acquisition of Avidity, Avidity holders received Atrium stock as consideration sweetener
and most sold immediately to lock in Novartis cash. Preclinical biotech with no revenue gets minimal coverage.
Inherited Avidity's old ticker (RNA) — confused traders who expected the parent's siRNA leadership think they
are buying the wrong thing.
EDGE
$270M cash on $220M market cap means investors are paid to own the platform; Kathleen Gallagher (former
Avidity CPO) brings real RNA-conjugate know-how. PLN cardiomyopathy and PRKAG2 syndrome have
demonstrable orphan-disease economics, and Novartis would be a logical re-acquirer if either lead progresses.
Asymmetric: small downside (cash floor), 5-10x upside on positive Phase 1 data.
catalyst:
• IND filings 2026-2027
• First Phase 1 dosing milestones
• Potential partnership or acquisition by Novartis or other RNA-therapeutic player
• Cash-burn discipline updates

name: Versigent PLC
thesis:
Brand-new spin (April 1, 2026) of Aptiv's electrical-distribution-systems business — global leader in vehicle
wiring/power architectures; classic 'too small to bother' setup right at maximum forced selling.
WHY MISPRICED
Spin completed less than 3 weeks ago — peak forced-selling window. Aptiv was held by tech/AI software/EV
growth funds who wanted the high-multiple S&S;/electronics business; the legacy wiring stub is anathema and
being sold indiscriminately. Versigent inherited $2.1B of new debt at distribution, scaring quality-focused buyers.
EDGE
100-year heritage as the dominant wiring-harness supplier with engineering centers on 4 continents and
manufacturing in 30+ countries — duopoly economics with Yazaki/Sumitomo; this is critical-path content per
vehicle and has structural growth as EV wiring complexity rises. CEO Joe Liotine has run the segment since 2024
and knows where margin can be added. Fresh spin, no analyst coverage yet — buy ahead of the initiation cycle.
catalyst:
• First standalone Q1/Q2 2026 earnings
• Sell-side analyst initiations
• Debt paydown demonstrating FCF conversion
• Settlement of forced-selling overhang
• Potential index inclusion 6-12 months out

name: GE Vernova Inc.
thesis:
Pure-play energy/grid equipment giant spun from GE — power, wind, electrification segments — became the
centerpiece of the AI-data-center grid build-out trade.
WHY MISPRICED
Originally spun at ~$141 with skepticism about wind losses and grid cyclicality; legacy GE holders rotated into
Aerospace and PE/quality holders had no model for a power-generation cyclical. Market still anchors on
early-2024 spin valuation while the order book has grown >$120B.
EDGE
Power and Electrification segments effectively own the choke point on grid transformer/turbine capacity for AI
data-center build-out; pricing power is real and sustained — gas-turbine slots are sold out into 2028. Wind losses
now narrowing while order backlog is locked in at much higher prices. Even after the run, forward EBITDA
bridge supports continued upside on margin expansion alone.
catalyst:
• Quarterly Power & Electrification orders updates
• Margin inflection in Wind segment
• Hyperscaler turbine reservation announcements
• Capital return ramp (buybacks/dividend)

name: Atmus Filtration Technologies, Inc.
thesis:
Pure-play industrial filtration leader (Fleetguard brand) split off from Cummins via exchange offer —
high-margin razor/razor-blade aftermarket model with consolidation runway in industrial air filtration.
WHY MISPRICED
Cummins holders received Atmus through an oversubscribed exchange offer that left an immediate float
overhang; quality-defense holders avoided it as a 'commoditized filter business' until they ran the numbers.
Long-term EV displacement risk is over-discounted relative to the 15-20 year tail of diesel fleets that need
consumables.
EDGE
Recently acquired Koch Filter for $450M to enter $13B industrial air filtration TAM with much better growth
profile; aftermarket revenue is ~80% of segment, recurring and pricing-led. 20% EBITDA margins, ~$160M
FCF, 2.0x leverage post-deal — clean compounder. Buybacks and bolt-on M&A; pipeline increasingly de-risk
catalyst:
• Quarterly Koch synergy updates
• Continued bolt-on M&A; in industrial air filtration
• Buyback authorization expansions
• Margin expansion into 22%+ range

name: Inhibrx Biosciences, Inc.
thesis:
Spun off from Inhibrx as part of Sanofi's $1.7B acquisition; received non-INBRX-101 pipeline (INBRX-109
chondrosarcoma, INBRX-1061) plus Sanofi/CVR economics; up 5x in 12 months and still under-followed.
WHY MISPRICED
Created via split-off transaction in conjunction with Sanofi M&A;, leaving forced-seller dynamics from arbs and
deal-funds; preclinical/early-clinical biotech with no revenue gets minimal sell-side coverage. Most former
Inhibrx holders just took the Sanofi cash and CVRs and moved on.
EDGE
INBRX-109 (chondrosarcoma DR5 agonist) has shown strong early single-arm Phase 2 data in conventional
chondrosarcoma — a rare cancer with no approved therapies; pivotal readout could trigger a buyout or
accelerated approval. Core team is a proven biotech operator (former Inhibrx mgmt). Still small enough that
single-asset success is transformative.
catalyst:
• INBRX-109 Phase 2 chondrosarcoma data updates
• Pivotal trial design announcement
• Strategic M&A; or partnership
• INBRX-1061 IND advancement

name: Sunrise Realty Trust, Inc.
thesis:
Externally managed CRE-debt REIT focused on Sun-Belt commercial real estate loans, spun from cannabis
lender AFC Gamma — micro-cap orphan with 14% dividend yield and clean balance sheet.
WHY MISPRICED
AFCG holders are cannabis-credit specialists who received a Sun-Belt CRE-mezz REIT they had zero appetite
for; immediate forced selling. ~$110M market cap is too small for almost any institutional benchmark. Externally
managed structure (Sunrise Manager LLC fee-drag) keeps quality investors away.
EDGE
Pure Sun-Belt CRE loan book at a time when distressed banks are exiting CRE lending — non-bank originators
with capital can pick over the carcass at high spreads. Dividend yield ~14% covered by current loan-book interest
income; loan portfolio is brand new (2024-2026 vintage), so credit risk is freshly underwritten. Tradable strategic
alternatives review opens the door to liquidation/rollup at NAV.
catalyst:
• Quarterly NII and originations updates
• Strategic alternatives review outcome
• Dividend coverage maintained or grown
• Loan-portfolio mark-to-market updates as Sun-Belt CRE stabilizes

name: Sirius XM Holdings Inc.
thesis:
'New Sirius' created by combining Liberty SiriusXM tracking stock with the public Sirius XM minority stake —
clean cap-table FCF compounder yielding ~5% with Buffett's Berkshire owning ~35%.
WHY MISPRICED
Tracking-stock split-off + immediate merger created confused supply/demand on the new ticker; legacy Liberty
holders sold tax-free and minorities received tiny stakes after a 1:10 conversion. Streaming-secular-decline
narrative dominates despite stable subscriber base and strong FCF.
EDGE
Berkshire's continued accumulation (now ~35%) provides downside support and validates intrinsic-value thesis;
clean post-split cap structure ($8.5B mcap) trades at <8x FCF with $1.2B annual FCF funding buybacks and ~5%
dividend. Auto-attach business stabilizing as new vehicle deliveries pick up; podcast/ad-supported tier expanding
TAM. Take-private optionality with Buffett at 35% is real.
catalyst:
• Quarterly FCF prints and capital returns
• Continued Berkshire purchases
• Subscriber stabilization in self-pay tier
• Potential strategic transaction or take-private

name: Brookfield Wealth Solutions Ltd.
thesis:
Brookfield's insurance/annuity arm (formerly Brookfield Reinsurance) re-branded with new ticker after
expanding via Just Group acquisition; share class exchangeable 1-for-1 into BN parent stock creates structural
arbitrage.
WHY MISPRICED
Listed on both NYSE and TSX under same ticker as a Bermuda-domiciled exchangeable share — confuses US
screens and excludes from Canadian-equity benchmarks. Insurance investors don't recognize it as a peer to
Athene/Global Atlantic; Brookfield investors view it as a sub-economic appendage. Frequent name changes
(BAMR → BNT) lost continuity in databases.
EDGE
Effectively a sponsor-backed alternative-asset insurance flywheel — generates ~$1.5B annual distributable
earnings funneled into Brookfield-managed yield strategies, recreating the Athene/Apollo model at a discount to
those peers. Just Group acquisition (UK retirement annuities) doubles AUM. Exchangeable into BN at 1:1 means
it can never trade meaningfully below BN's per-share value — embedded floor.
catalyst:
• Quarterly distributable-earnings growth
• Continued AUM/insurance-block acquisitions
• Convergence trade as US dual-listing mechanics get understood

name: Smithfield Foods, Inc.
thesis:
Largest US pork producer carved-out from Hong Kong-listed WH Group via Nasdaq IPO/partial spin; ~88% still
owned by WH Group, creating an orphan-like float despite a $11B mcap.
WHY MISPRICED
IPO priced below range ($20 vs $23-27 indicated) due to Chinese parent overhang and investor concerns about
pork-cycle timing. WH Group still owns ~88% so float is small (~12%) and trades like a micro-cap despite the
mcap. Most US food generalists don't include foreign-controlled food processors in their universe.
EDGE
Trades at ~10x P/E with 9.8% YoY revenue growth into 2025 ($15.5B), record FCF, and a $1.3B Sioux Falls
capacity expansion announced; recent Nathan's Famous acquisition at 12.4x EBITDA is immediately accretive
and signals disciplined capital allocation. Continued WH Group secondary sales will increase float and trigger
index inclusion. Strong FCF supports buyback authorization.
catalyst:
• Continued WH Group secondary share sales
• Nathan's Famous deal close
• S&P; MidCap inclusion as float grows
• Margin recovery on hog cycle inflection

name: Resolute Holdings Management, Inc.
thesis:
David Cote/Tom Knott-led alternative asset manager spun from CompoSecure to manage CMPO and future
bolt-ons via long-term operating-services agreement — public 'Brookfield/3G-style' platform at sub-scale.
WHY MISPRICED
Tiny float (one share per 12 CMPO held), no revenue/profit at spin, taxable distribution drove indiscriminate
selling. Early skepticism of fee-only platform with one client (CompoSecure). Massive run since spin (24x off
initial $24 lows to $147) means most analysts now consider it 'too expensive', missing the optionality on adding
new managed companies.
EDGE
David Cote (former Honeywell CEO) is one of the most credible operator-CEOs of his generation; Tom Knott has
a strong CompoSecure track record. Vehicle is structured to acquire and operate businesses for fees + carry —
early-stage alts platform analogous to Brookfield Asset Management or Constellation Software at a much smaller
starting base. Each new managed acquisition compounds the fee base.
catalyst:
• First operating-services revenue print
• Announcement of new managed acquisitions
• Initiation of analyst coverage
• Capital raise to fund managed-company acquisitions

name: EuroHoldings Ltd.
thesis:
Greek shipping micro-cap spun from Euroseas with two older feeder containerships and no debt; immediately
initiated strategic alternatives review and pivoted toward MR product tankers — pure NAV/SOTP arbitrage.
WHY MISPRICED
Spun off as a debt-free entity with two older containerships at a time of rising containership values, but at <$30M
market cap is invisible to institutions. Greek-shipping orphan structure with confusing ticker history
(ESEA→EHLD spin); strategic-alternatives review announcement six weeks after spin signals deeply discounted
NAV.
EDGE
NAV substantially exceeds market cap based on second-hand vessel values plus cash; new pivot to product
tankers (MR segment) acquired Hellas Avatar in late 2025 — aligns with 2026 product-tanker supply/demand
inflection. Strategic alternatives review could result in special dividend, liquidation, or sale to peer. Asymmetric:
small downside (vessel scrap value floor) with multi-bagger NAV upside.
catalyst:
• Strategic alternatives review outcome
• Vessel sales or fleet additions
• Special dividend declarations
• Tanker market re-rating in 2026

name: Robin Energy Ltd.
thesis:
Single-vessel Handysize tanker company spun from Toro Corp; same Petros Panagiotidis Greek-shipping family
running both — hyper-micro-cap orphan trading near vessel value.
WHY MISPRICED
Toro shareholders received 1 RBNE share per 8 Toro shares — economically dilutive distribution that drove
dumping. Started with literally one Handysize tanker; <$10M mcap so invisible. Same controlling shareholder as
parent (Panagiotidis) creates governance discount.
EDGE
Vessel revenues grew 3.3x year-over-year through 2025 as Handysize charter rates strengthened; Q4 2025 vessel
revenues hit $4.3M vs $1.3M prior year. Fleet expansion ongoing (Robin's 9-month 2025 vessel revenues $9.9M,
up 45.6%). Trades near scrap+iron value with tanker upside; tiny float means any institutional interest moves
price violently.
catalyst:
• Quarterly vessel revenue and fleet additions
• Tanker market re-rating in 2026
• Strategic combination with parent Toro
• Special dividend / liquidity event

name: Costamare Bulkers Holdings Limited
thesis:
Pure-play dry-bulk shipping company spun from container-shipper Costamare; new vehicle inherits CMRE's
dry-bulk fleet and trading platform.
WHY MISPRICED
CMRE holders are container-shipping investors who didn't want dry bulk exposure; 1-for-5 distribution ratio
drove mass forced selling at the spin. Greek-shipping orphan with sub-$500M float makes it uninvestable for
most institutions. Currently below NAV based on vessel values.
EDGE
Dry-bulk vessel values have rallied through 2025-2026 on China stimulus and grain trade rerouting; CMDB's
fleet was contributed at conservative book values, leaving NAV optionality. Asset-light trading platform within
CMDB drives capital-efficient earnings power separate from owned fleet. Same Konstantakopoulos family
aligned alongside minority shareholders.
catalyst:
• Quarterly TCE rate and trading-platform updates
• Dry-bulk cycle re-rating
• Vessel-value mark-to-market disclosures
• Strategic combination or take-private

name: Rubico Inc.
thesis:
Two modern Suezmax tankers (~157k DWT each, scrubber-equipped) spun from TOP Ships into a clean entity;
nano-cap with current TCE rates plus IMO 2030 carbon-intensity tailwinds.
WHY MISPRICED
TOPS shareholders got Rubico in a tiny pro-rata distribution that immediately got dumped. Recent 1-for-10
reverse split reset price optics and likely scrubbed retail interest. Sub-$3M market cap is unfollowable but vessel
NAV is ~$120M+ for two modern scrubber Suezmaxes — extreme NAV gap.
EDGE
Two Suezmax scrubber-equipped tankers with eco-design have premium TCE rates in any constructive
crude-tanker market; 2026-2030 IMO carbon-intensity rules continue to advantage modern scrubber tonnage.
NAV/share is dramatically higher than market price; any liquidity event (vessel sale, M&A; combo, special
dividend) is materially accretive. Lottery-ticket style asymmetry.
catalyst:
• First standalone TCE rate prints
• Vessel sale or combination announcement
• Tanker market re-rating
• Special dividend or share buyback

name: Canada Packers Inc.
thesis:
Maple 	Leaf 	Foods' 	pork 	operations 	spun 	out 	into 	Canada 	Packers 	— 	North 	America's 	largest
raised-without-antibiotics (RWA) pork producer with integrated supply chain, US-traded via OTC.
WHY MISPRICED
TSX-primary listing under 'CPKR' with US OTC pink ticker 'CPKRF' creates orphan-status — invisible to most
US screens and excluded from Canadian small-cap benchmarks. Maple Leaf retained 16% so float is small.
Pork-cycle bearish positioning post-Tyson and Smithfield troubles depresses valuation despite RWA
differentiation.
EDGE
Premium 'raised without antibiotics' positioning attracts higher-value retail and foodservice contracts; integrated
hog production through processing creates margin stability. Evergreen supply agreement with Maple Leaf
provides downstream visibility. Trades at material discount to US-listed pork peers (SFD) on EBITDA. Capacity
for capital returns once standalone reporting begins.
catalyst:
• First quarterly standalone print
• US dual-listing on NYSE/Nasdaq
• Margin expansion as RWA pricing premium scales
• Strategic interest from larger US/global pork players

name: AUMOVIO SE (ADR)
thesis:
Continental's automotive electronics, sensors, ADAS, and software-defined vehicle business demerged into
standalone AUMOVIO; ¤1.5B starting cash + ¤2.5B credit line; trades as US ADR (AMVOY).
WHY MISPRICED
European-listed (FRA: AUM) with US ADR creates dual-orphan status; Continental holders are tire/rubber
investors who don't want auto-tech exposure. Valued at only ¤3.7B at launch despite ¤19.4B revenue (~0.2x
sales) 	— 	depressed 	by 	general 	European 	auto 	pessimism. 	Slow-growth 	ADAS 	narrative 	ignores
software-defined-vehicle inflection.
EDGE
Critical-content ADAS/sensors/SDV supplier to global OEMs at a time when those OEMs are racing to
commodify and outsource the software stack — AUMOVIO has the IP and engineering to be the 'Tier 0.5'
partner. Capacity to scale to ¤24B revenue, currently trading at <0.25x sales vs Aptiv at 1x+. Net cash gives
flexibility for M&A; or buybacks. Re-rating thesis on first standalone investor day.
catalyst:
• First standalone Q4 2025 / Q1 2026 results
• ADAS/SDV design-win announcements
• Capital-allocation framework (M&A;, buybacks)
• Index inclusion in DAX or SXXP

name: Liberty Live Holdings, Inc. (Series A)
thesis:
Liberty's 30% Live Nation stake re-housed as a standalone holding company via split-off — eliminates
tracking-stock NAV discount and unlocks direct ownership of LYV stake plus Quint and minority investments.
WHY MISPRICED
Tracking-stock conversion to asset-backed split-off completed mid-December 2025; many tax-loss sellers and
ETF rebalancers dumped at year-end without considering the structural NAV discount narrowing. Even at $92,
still trades at a material discount to LYV per-share value (look-through equity).
EDGE
Direct ownership of 69.6M Live Nation shares (~$22B at recent LYV pricing) means the embedded NAV is well
above the current $8.6B market cap — historic Liberty discount of 25-35% for tracking stock should compress
meaningfully now that it's a real cap-stock. John Malone's Liberty playbook has rewarded patient holders for
decades. Buybacks and special distributions likely.
catalyst:
• Buyback authorization and execution
• Continued Live Nation outperformance
• Potential Greg Maffei capital allocation actions
• Additional minority investments monetized

name: Janus Living Inc.
thesis:
First pure-play, fully RIDEA-structured publicly-traded senior-housing REIT carve-out from Healthpeak (DOC);
~10,400 units, $878M IPO proceeds, 85% still held by Healthpeak.
WHY MISPRICED
IPO/carve-out structure (rather than pure spin) created mixed institutional reception; Healthpeak retains ~85%, so
float is small. Senior-housing sector still shadowed by COVID-era operating issues despite occupancy recovery.
Newly created vehicle with limited trading history and no analyst coverage at launch.
EDGE
Only US-listed pure-play senior-housing REIT under fully RIDEA structure means upside from operating
recovery flows directly to shareholders; sector has multi-year demographic tailwind from aging Baby Boomers.
Recent Atlanta-area $240M acquisition demonstrates external growth runway. Healthpeak's gradual sell-down
will boost float and trigger index inclusion. RIDEA structure should re-rate to NHC/SBRA premium.
catalyst:
• Healthpeak secondary share sales increasing float
• First standalone earnings demonstrating same-property NOI growth
• Index inclusion as float seasons
• Continued external acquisitions at attractive cap rates

name: Twin Hospitality Group Inc.
thesis:
Twin Peaks + Smokey Bones casual-dining spinoff from FAT Brands — now in Chapter 11 (April 2026) creating
a deep distressed/post-bankruptcy equity stub setup.
WHY MISPRICED
Spun at $17.45, collapsed 99.5% over 12 months as same-store sales deteriorated and debt overhang took hold;
subsequently filed Chapter 11. FAT Brands still owns ~95% so true float is tiny. Current micro-cap is essentially
pricing zero recovery for equity post-restructuring — but Twin Peaks brand and ~85 units have real franchise
value.
EDGE
Distressed-equity playbook: Chapter 11 reorganization will likely wipe out current equity but provides clean
optionality on a recovery scenario where Twin Peaks brand is sold to PE or another franchisor at strategic
multiples. Smokey Bones conversion runway adds optionality. For micro-cap distressed-debt funds, the
post-emergence equity could be very interesting.
catalyst:
• Chapter 11 plan of reorganization filings
• Brand sale or restructuring announcements
• Post-emergence equity issuance terms
• FAT Brands strategic decision on residual stake

name: Grail, Inc.
thesis:
Multi-cancer early-detection (MCED) liquid-biopsy company spun from Illumina under FTC/EU regulatory
pressure; pure-play on Galleri commercial ramp with $1B+ Illumina-funded balance sheet at spin.
WHY MISPRICED
Forced spin from Illumina under regulatory pressure, not strategic logic, created indiscriminate selling on receipt;
Illumina holders are sequencing-tools investors with no appetite for an early-stage diagnostic company. Galleri
reimbursement/coverage timeline is uncertain, creating bear-case noise that drowns out commercial progress.
EDGE
Galleri (multi-cancer early-detection blood test) addresses a >$50B TAM with growing payer interest as coverage
decisions land in 2026-2027; Illumina contributed $1B+ at spin to fund 4-5 years of cash burn. Strategic acquirers
(large diagnostics companies, pharma with oncology screening) likely to circle if data continues to validate.
Recent positive Medicare draft signals add asymmetric upside.
catalyst:
• Medicare/CMS coverage decisions on Galleri
• Pivotal commercial data updates
• Strategic M&A; interest
• Population-health pilot expansions

name: Solventum Corporation
thesis:
3M's healthcare business (medical, dental, health-info systems, purification) spun off into Solventum —
leveraged carve-out with margin-expansion and divestiture optionality.
WHY MISPRICED
3M's mass distribution to dividend/quality holders led to immediate selling pressure; high net leverage at spin
(~3.5x) and the optical 'bad-cousin' label vs 3M's industrial purity scared off quality investors. Stagnant top-line
growth narrative ignored the impending divestiture of low-growth purification business.
EDGE
Just announced sale of Purification & Filtration to Thermo Fisher for $4.1B — proceeds slash leverage, allow
management to focus on core medical/dental segments where margins are 25%+. Standalone capital allocation,
simplified portfolio, and ~$2B annual FCF support meaningful equity re-rating; consensus PT $80-90 vs current
~$70.
catalyst:
• Purification divestiture close and debt paydown
• Margin-expansion targets at 2026 investor day
• Buyback initiation post-deal
• Multi-year revenue growth re-acceleration in medical surgical

name: Worthington Steel, Inc.
thesis:
Steel processing spin from Worthington Enterprises — overlooked play on electrical-steel laminations for
transformers and EV traction motors at a time of structural transformer shortage.
WHY MISPRICED
Worthington Enterprises (consumer/building products) holders sold the cyclical steel-processing stub; <$2B
market cap is sub-scale for major steel funds (NUE/CLF/STLD). Recent quarter-by-quarter volatility from
auto-cycle exposure obscures the multi-year electrical-steel growth story.
EDGE
Electrical-steel laminations for transformers and EV motors are structurally undersupplied (2-year transformer
backlog cited by management); $85M Mexico + $85M Canada capacity additions come online 2026. Recent
Sitem (Italy) acquisition adds European motor-lamination foothold. Trades at ~14x P/E vs strategic value of
electrical-steel franchise — strategic-acquirer interest from Cleveland-Cliffs or NSC realistic.
catalyst:
• Quarterly electrical-steel revenue mix updates
• Mexico/Canada capacity ramp in 2026
• Sitem integration accretion
• Strategic M&A; interest

name: Crane NXT, Co.
thesis:
Spun from Crane Holdings to be the pure-play Payment Innovations + Security/Authentication Technologies
business — niche industrial-tech compounder with hidden currency-printing utility.
WHY MISPRICED
Crane Holdings holders are diversified industrial investors who didn't want a payment/security niche stub;
ongoing legacy-cash-handling pressure (declining cash usage in some markets) keeps generalist multiples
compressed. Most recent quarter showed 19.5% revenue growth with non-GAAP EPS beating consensus, but
stock still trades at ~13x earnings — well below industrial-tech peers.
EDGE
Federal Reserve 2026 print order projects banknote demand of 3.8-5.1 billion units — high-single-digit US
currency revenue growth. Authentication and brand-protection segment is a high-margin oligopoly with growing
TAM as luxury and pharmaceutical authentication scales. Active in tuck-in M&A; clean balance sheet supports
buybacks. EV/EBITDA ~9x vs industrial-tech peers at 13-15x.
catalyst:
• Quarterly Fed print order updates
• Authentication segment growth momentum
• Tuck-in M&A; in security/authentication
• Buyback acceleration

name: PHINIA Inc.
thesis:
Fuel-systems and aftermarket business spun from BorgWarner; orphaned by EV-narrative funds despite strong
recurring aftermarket FCF and commercial-vehicle/aftermarket exposure that's structurally insulated from EV
transition.
WHY MISPRICED
BorgWarner holders rotated cleanly into the EV-pure-play parent; PHIN was viewed as 'stranded ICE asset' and
dumped indiscriminately. Internal-combustion narrative blinds investors to the 15-20 year tail of diesel
commercial-vehicles and the high-margin global aftermarket. Light analyst coverage relative to size.
EDGE
Aftermarket revenue is recurring high-margin, ~30% of business; commercial-vehicle and industrial customers
will burn diesel for decades. ~$130M trailing net income on $3.5B revenue with capacity for buybacks and 2-3%
dividend. Trades at ~9x forward EPS vs industrial peers at 12-15x. Capital-allocation discipline (buybacks +
bolt-on M&A;) closing the gap.
catalyst:
• Quarterly aftermarket revenue and margin updates
• Buyback acceleration
• Hydrogen/alternative-fuel injection-system wins (announced 2025)
• Strategic combination with another aftermarket player

name: Veralto Corporation
thesis:
Danaher's Environmental & Applied Solutions segment (water analytics + product quality/marking) spun out —
Danaher-quality compounder DNA at lower starting valuation, still under-followed.
WHY MISPRICED
Danaher holders kept the higher-multiple bioprocess parent and were ambivalent on the 'old industrial' E&AS;
stub; spun at a discount to peer ROP/BIO-Techne/IEX. Despite Danaher Business System DNA, market priced
VLTO as a generic industrial-tools company.
EDGE
VLTO inherited Danaher's M&A; playbook and capital-allocation rigor; water-quality segment has strong
recurring-consumables economics and structural reshoring tailwinds. Product Quality & Innovation includes
Videojet/X-Rite — high-margin franchises in adjacent spaces. Well below DHR-style multiple despite identical
operating model. Even modest multiple expansion to peer levels = ~30% upside.
catalyst:
• Quarterly margin and DBS-driven productivity updates
• Bolt-on M&A; in water analytics
• Buyback authorization expansions
• Multi-year reshoring water-treatment tailwind

name: WK Kellogg Co
thesis:
North American cereal pure-play spun from Kellogg; subsequently agreed to be acquired by Ferrero — classic
spin-then-merger value crystallization story still playing out.
WHY MISPRICED
Kellogg holders rotated to global snacks (Kellanova/K); KLG was treated as a declining North American cereal
stub trading at ~5x EBITDA. Ferrero deal (announced 2025) changed the calculus but spread to deal price reflects
ongoing antitrust risk and timing uncertainty.
EDGE
Ferrero deal at $23/share provides hard deal-price floor; antitrust process is expected to close 2H 2026 with high
probability of approval. Standalone, brands like Frosted Flakes, Froot Loops have demonstrated pricing power.
For arb funds, the spread-to-deal is attractive risk/reward; for fundamental holders, the deal validates intrinsic
value the public market wouldn't recognize.
catalyst:
• Antitrust approvals and deal close
• Quarterly earnings showing sustained margin
• Deal-spread compression as close approaches
• Possible bidding war if Mars or other strategic emerges

name: Atlanta Braves Holdings, Inc.
thesis:
Atlanta Braves MLB franchise + Battery Atlanta mixed-use development split off from Liberty Media — only
pure-play publicly traded MLB team trading at material discount to franchise value.
WHY MISPRICED
Tracking stock conversion to a real-asset stub created confused float; baseball/sports franchise valuation methods
aren't standard among generalist equity funds. MLB media-rights uncertainty and team-payroll concerns weighed
on multiples. Mixed-use real estate development underwrites partly hidden by sports-team cash-flow noise.
EDGE
Recent MLB franchise sales (Orioles ~$1.7B, Twins talks ~$1.7B) imply Braves franchise alone is worth $3-4B;
The Battery Atlanta mixed-use complex adds another $500M-$800M of real-asset value. Combined SOTP
suggests $4-5B+ NAV vs $3B mcap. Liberty/Malone has historically rewarded patient holders with eventual sale
or special distribution. Tax-efficient sale to billionaire buyer remains the bull-case endgame.
catalyst:
• Comparable MLB franchise sale prints
• Battery Atlanta development monetization
• MLB media-rights renewal
• Eventual sale of franchise to billionaire bidder

name: SBC Medical Group Holdings Inc.
thesis:
Japan-based aesthetic-medicine MSO carved out via SPAC/listing process onto Nasdaq; franchise services across
cosmetic surgery, dermatology, fertility — global expansion underway.
WHY MISPRICED
Listed via SPAC merger / carve-out path with HeartCore Go-IPO consulting structure — confusing legal heritage
for US institutional investors. Japan-domiciled with majority foreign-controlled ownership creates governance
discount. Aesthetic medicine viewed as discretionary, not staple — over-discounted in current macro.
EDGE
Largest aesthetic-medicine MSO in Japan with mid-teens revenue growth and high-30s EBITDA margins;
expanding into US and Asia franchise base. $50M shelf + buyback announced shows management confidence. As
foreign-issuer reporting normalizes and US analyst coverage initiates, multiple expansion likely. Profitable
medical-services compounder rarely stays this cheap.
catalyst:
• Continued US/Asia franchise expansion announcements
• Buyback execution updates
• Initiation of US analyst coverage
• Possible secondary listing on Tokyo or Singapore

name: Sandoz Group AG (ADR)
thesis:
Global generics + biosimilars leader spun from Novartis; trades on SIX as primary listing with US ADR
(SDZNY) on OTCQX — index-orphan on US side and structurally undervalued.
WHY MISPRICED
Listed on SIX Switzerland with only OTCQX ADR in the US — excluded from US benchmarks (S&P; 500,
Russell). Novartis ADR holders received Sandoz ADRs but most sold immediately to remain in pure
innovative-Pharma exposure. Generics narrative is bearish despite strong biosimilars TAM growth.
EDGE
Biosimilars business has multi-year tailwind from Humira/Enbrel/Stelara biosimilar launches across global
markets; pricing structures more durable than 'commodity generics' bear case suggests. Strong cash flow funding
deleveraging plus dividend; >100% stock appreciation since spin yet still trades at <12x forward EPS vs
branded-pharma peers at 15-18x. Catalysts on US listing and index inclusion would re-rate.
catalyst:
• Biosimilar launch revenue ramps
• Quarterly margin and FCF updates
• Possible US dual-listing on NYSE/Nasdaq
• Strategic combinations or licensing deals

name: Knife River Corporation
thesis:
Aggregates and construction-materials business spun from MDU Resources — pure infrastructure/IIJA play with
regional pricing power on aggregates, sand, gravel, and ready-mix.
WHY MISPRICED
MDU utility/dividend holders sold the cyclical construction-materials stub; original spin valuation reflected
pre-IIJA-ramp expectations. Acquisition-driven growth strategy (buying regional aggregates operators) was
viewed skeptically until the deals proved accretive.
EDGE
Regional aggregates business has near-monopoly pricing in many western US markets; IIJA + state-and-local
infrastructure capex are multi-year tailwinds materializing through 2026-2028. M&A; roll-up of regional players
accretive to per-share earnings; strong FCF generation funds both buybacks and continued bolt-ons.
Strategic-acquirer interest from CRH/Vulcan/Martin Marietta is realistic given concentration of attractive pits.
catalyst:
• Quarterly aggregates pricing and volume updates
• M&A; pipeline announcements
• IIJA project award flow
• Possible strategic-acquirer interest

name: GE HealthCare Technologies Inc.
thesis:
Imaging + ultrasound + patient-monitoring + pharmaceutical-diagnostics pure-play spun from GE; #1 in MR/CT
imaging globally with multi-year backlog.
WHY MISPRICED
First leg of GE breakup, distributed to a broad GE shareholder base that included many non-healthcare-focused
funds; 	sold-out 	indiscriminately 	at 	spin. 	Hospital 	capex 	pause 	narratives 	over-discounted 	long-term
imaging-volume growth. Smaller analyst following relative to peers SYK/MDT/PHG.
EDGE
Backlog of over $19B with improving book-to-bill; AI-enabled imaging products driving share gains. PDx
(pharma diagnostics) segment is high-margin recurring contrast-agent business. Margin expansion ongoing as
standalone management drives portfolio rationalization. Trades at meaningful discount to medical-device peers
despite market-leading positions and accelerating procedure volumes.
catalyst:
• Quarterly book-to-bill updates
• AI-imaging product launches
• Margin-expansion targets
• Bolt-on M&A; in software/AI imaging

name: Kellanova
thesis:
Global snacking + international cereal + plant-based + frozen foods (Pringles, Cheez-It, Eggo) — agreed to be
acquired by Mars at $83.50 cash; arb spread + closing optionality.
WHY MISPRICED
Mars deal announced at $83.50 cash provides hard ceiling; spread to deal reflects antitrust uncertainty (especially
in snack-bar/cracker overlap). Some legacy Kellogg holders had to sell on receipt due to mandate restrictions on
foreign-controlled or PE-controlled investments.
EDGE
Mars deal is materially advanced through US/EU antitrust review and on track to close mid-2026; deal arbitrage
offers attractive annualized yield to close. Standalone, Kellanova has demonstrated mid-single-digit organic
growth and pricing power post-COVID. Even if deal breaks, fundamental value supports current trading price.
Asymmetric risk/reward favors holders.
catalyst:
• Antitrust approvals (EU, US, China)
• Deal close expected mid-2026
• Possible competing bid
• Standalone performance updates if deal extends

name: Atlanta Braves Holdings, Inc. (Series C)
thesis:
Series C non-voting class of Atlanta Braves Holdings; trades at a small discount to BATRA (Series A voting) —
pair-trade or value-conscious-entry alternative for the same MLB franchise SOTP thesis.
WHY MISPRICED
Two share classes (BATRA voting + BATRK non-voting) create persistent small spread that retail/smaller
institutions don't arbitrage; non-voting structure depresses BATRK relative to BATRA despite identical economic
interest. Same underlying tracking-stock-conversion forced selling drove both lower than franchise NAV.
EDGE
Identical economic exposure to Atlanta Braves franchise + Battery Atlanta as BATRA, but at a slight discount; in
a take-private scenario, both classes would receive same per-share consideration — discount disappears. Same
MLB franchise NAV thesis ($4-5B+) applies. Larger float in BATRK provides better liquidity for institutional
catalyst:
• Same as BATRA: MLB franchise sale comps, Battery monetization, eventual sale
• Voting/non-voting class collapse if take-private occurs
• MLB media-rights renewal

name: Liberty Live Holdings, Inc. (Series C)
thesis:
Non-voting Series C of Liberty Live Holdings; same Live Nation 30% stake exposure as LLYVA at modest
discount — pair-trade and SOTP play.
WHY MISPRICED
Two-class spin structure creates persistent small spread; LLYVK (Series C, non-voting) trades at slight discount
to LLYVA despite same economic interest. Recent split-off completed December 2025 still in 'forced selling'
window from former Liberty Media holders.
EDGE
Direct exposure to 69.6M Live Nation shares (~$22B) at a holdco that trades meaningfully below look-through
NAV; LLYVK provides marginally cheaper entry to the same compounding thesis. Larger float vs LLYVA
provides better liquidity for institutional sized positions. Buybacks, special distributions, or eventual collapse of
dual-class structure are all upside catalysts.
catalyst:
• Same as LLYVA: buybacks, Live Nation outperformance, special distributions
• Dual-class structure collapse possibility
• Index inclusion as post-spin seasoning completes

and consumer/non-resources exposure. Buffett still buying. Aggressive buybacks (1.4T yen authorized 2024-25).
FamilyMart take-private (2020) was first SOTP catalyst; further consumer carve-outs possible. ADR (ITOCY)
avoids Japan-direct settlement complexity.
catalyst:
• Continued Berkshire accumulation
• Larger buybacks (Tokyo Stock Exchange 'PBR<1' campaign)
• Consumer carve-outs (Dole, FamilyMart partials)
• Yen normalization

name: Mitsubishi Corporation (Buffett's largest sogo
thesis:

discount to Morningstar fair value SOTP; mega-deal Aethon shale acquisition demonstrates capital deployment.
WHY MISPRICED
Resource exposure perceived as cyclical (commodity prices); Japan listing premium not yet fully closed despite
Buffett halo; sogo shosha business model opaque to Western analysts; FX (JPY) drag; regulatory environment in
resource-heavy regions.
EDGE
Berkshire 10.23% stake (and growing) provides validation. Largest, most diversified, lowest-cost-of-capital sogo
shosha. Aethon shale deal expands US exposure. TSE 'PBR<1' campaign and management mid-term plan (MC
Shared Value 2024) commit to 9-10% ROE and aggressive buybacks. ADR (MSBHF) tradeable for US investors
despite low daily volume; main listing 8058.T highly liquid.
catalyst:
• Berkshire continues accumulation
• Aethon integration 2026-2027
• Continued buybacks (1T+ JPY YTD)
• Lawson take-private completed 2024 (Saulnier)

discount.
EDGE
Wealthsimple is the hidden gem -- Canada's leading digital broker/wealth manager, profitable 2024, valuations of
comparable fintechs (Robinhood ~25x earnings) imply C$12-15B+ value, captured at conservative book in NAV.
New CEO (Jeffrey Orr through 2024) shareholder-aligned. Aggressive buybacks. Discount has narrowed from
30% historical to 20% but room for further compression with Wealthsimple monetization.
catalyst:

• GWL/IGM continued earnings growth
• Sagard alt-asset AUM scaling
• Buyback acceleration

past; complex Schwartz multiple-voting shares (still founder-controlled though Schwartz transitioning);
slow-realizations narrative.
EDGE
$8B realizations in 2025 ($800m to Onex) demonstrates exit machine working. Management explicitly identifies
30-40 point discount as anomalous; aggressive buyback commitments. Gerry Schwartz stepping back to chairman
(succession to Bobby Le Blanc as CEO 2024); cleaner story. Convex Insurance IPO/sale potential = major
catalyst:

name: Seaboard Corporation (Bresky family)

14% interest in Butterball, full ownership of Seaboard Marine, Seaboard Foods (#3 US pork), Seaboard Overseas
Trading.
WHY MISPRICED
Pork/protein cycle severely depressed 2023-2025; sugar/ethanol cyclically weak; Bresky family >70% control
limits institutional ownership; tiny float; minimal sell-side coverage (~zero analyst); $2,350+ share price ($2k+
per-share) deters retail; complexity across 6 segments.
EDGE
Trades at deepest discount to underlying assets in 40 years per Seeking Alpha analysis. Each segment in cyclical
trough simultaneously -- statistically rare. Book value ~$4,500/share with conservative marks. Massive cash and
securities portfolio at HoldCo (~$700m). Pork prices began recovering Q4 2025-Q1 2026. Patient money: Bresky
family rarely sells but buybacks have happened at depressed levels.
catalyst:
• Pork cycle recovery 2026-2027
• Sugar/ethanol normalization
• Possible Butterball IPO/sale (Smithfield take-private overhang)
• Latam containerized shipping demand recovery

name: Dundee Corporation (Goodman family mining

EDGE
Jonathan Goodman (son of founder) executing turnaround; multiple 2025-2026 monetizations crystallized cash.
New Found Gold unit sale + Borborema royalty sale = $200m+ cash in 12 months for $95m mcap company. Even
at deep discount, mathematics favor patience. 'Mining platform' strategy may attract third-party LP capital. Highly
asymmetric.

Oncology stake, and other juniors. Royalty interests including the Borborema Gold royalty (sold to Gold Royalty
Corp Jan 2026 for US$45m). Westhaven Gold $85m JV/earn-in (Shovelnose). United Hydrocarbon (private).
AgriMarine (aquaculture). Cash from monetizations.
